name: Western Union
description_short: San Francisco, CA's Western Union location at 1301 Market Street offers money transfers, bill payments, and check cashing services daily 8AM-9PM.
description_long:
Western Union's San Francisco location sits at 1301 Market Street in the heart of downtown, just blocks from the Ferry Building and BART stations. This standalone storefront operates 7 days a week from 8AM to 9PM, making it accessible for quick money transfer and check-cashing needs. Whether you're a resident navigating San Francisco's financial services or visiting the area, this Market Street location offers convenient weekday and weekend hours.

The 1301 Market Street branch in San Francisco, CA handles domestic and international money transfers, bill payments, and check cashing with competitive rates. Call +1 415-861-4010 to confirm rates or ask about specific transfer options. Staff can assist with wire transfers to anywhere in the world and local bill payments.

For San Francisco residents and visitors, bring a valid government-issued ID for any transaction—it's required. This location is ideal during off-hours when most banks are closed, especially since it's open until 9PM most evenings. Western Union's network makes it simple to send or receive money without needing a bank account.
pros: Extensive agent network with 150+ locations shown in Colorado alone, integrated into grocery stores and retailers for convenience
24/7 online money transfer capability with in-store pickup based on agent hours
Multilingual interface support including English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, and Vietnamese
Multiple service options including wire transfers, money orders, bill payment, mobile top-up, and inmate payment
Western Union Rewards program for transaction incentives
Ability to receive money on a card and track transfers in real-time
No requirement for traditional banking account to send or receive money
cons: Website content does not specify transfer fees, exchange rates, or pricing transparency
Service temporarily unavailable for certain payment methods (Orange wallet noted as unavailable during documentation)
Limited to transaction services only—no credit products, loans, or savings accounts offered
Dependent on agent location hours for cash pickup; not truly 24/7 for in-person transactions
No information provided about customer support responsiveness or dispute resolution timelines
best_for: Consumers sending international remittances to family members abroad
Individuals needing quick cash access without a traditional bank account
People paying bills or sending money to incarcerated individuals
Customers in underbanked communities relying on retail agent locations
diagnosis: Western Union is best for consumers who need quick money movement, international remittances, or cash access through convenient retail locations without a bank account. The primary caveat is that Western Union is purely a transaction facilitator—it offers no credit products, savings accounts, or financial building tools, making it unsuitable for credit repair, debt management, or loan purposes.
services: Online money transfer with in-store payment options
meta_description: Western Union at 1301 Market Street, San Francisco, CA. Money transfers, bill payments, and check cashing services open daily 8AM-9PM.
